description: GET /lineages/{lineage_id}/current returns the newest NON-SUPERSEDED version. status: expired counts as non-superseded (a valid current head); status: superseded never is. If every version is superseded (an abnormal state), the endpoint MUST return not_found. RFC-ACDP-0004 §5.2.

lineages.note: Abnormal state: every version is superseded (reachable only via admin correction or data corruption).
Current head is past its expires_at but has no successor — status expired.
Ordinary state: current head is active.

scenarios.name: All versions superseded — current returns not_found
scenarios.notes: No non-superseded version exists. The registry MUST return not_found (HTTP 404). It MUST NOT fall back to returning a superseded version as the current head. Registries SHOULD prevent this state via the supersession constraints of RFC-ACDP-0003 §3.1.

scenarios.name: Expired head — current returns the expired context with status: expired
scenarios.notes: An expired version is NOT superseded — it is a valid current head. The response MUST carry registry_state.status = expired so the consumer knows the context is past its validity window. 'current' does not imply 'active'.

scenarios.name: Active head — current returns the active context
scenarios.notes: The ordinary case: the newest non-superseded version is active and is returned as the current head.
